title: Hattie and the Tower of Traffic Cones
summary: Hattie the Mouse is enjoying a quiet lunch in her tree-stump workshop when she is visited by Mischief Mouse and a massive group of orange traffic cones with unique names. The cones spend their afternoon running circles around the workshop and dancing to the 'Caribbean Amphibian' song. Under the guidance of Mischief Mouse, the cones learn how to stack themselves into an incredibly tall tower. As the day ends, the cones hop down and magically transform into flowers, teddy bears, and carrots before finally heading home.
audioFilename: Stars and stories - Hattie and the Cones.m4a

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who is going to be in our story tonight?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie the mouse again. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was in her workshop having a nice quiet lunch all by herself. And all of a sudden she heard...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Knock knock knock knocking.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No knocking? Oh, she heard squeak squeak squeak squeak squeak. It was a funny sound.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it was squeak and it was Mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was Mischief Mouse squeaking. And Mischief Mouse had a whole big group of new friends with her and Hattie the mouse opened the door and said, 'Oh hello Mischief Mouse. Who are all of these orange cone-shaped friends you've got with you?' And what did Mischief Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Porpy and Purpy and Row and Toms and Bruly and Chog.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Those are their names? Wow, I'm not going to remember all those names.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Bolly and Dolly and Tolly and Pam and Dollyums and Jollie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mischief Mouse said 'Yeah, those are their names. These are my friends the Traffic Cones.' And Hattie the mouse said 'Wow, I've never met a traffic cone before. Um, what should we do? I don't even know. Do traffic cones eat lunch? Do you want to come in for lunch? Do they like to wear hats? Could they be hats?' And what did Mischief Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They're not hats, they're people.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They're people? Oh, I didn't know traffic cones were people. Well, what should we do together?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the traffic cone said, 'Well we'd like to get some energy out. Do you have some ideas for us Hattie the mouse?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Running around.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Running around? Okay, let's try it. And so how did they run around?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Running noises) Run run run run run run!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh they dashed! They ran in a little circle. Zoom zoom zoom zoom zoom. All the traffic cones went really fast in a circle all the way around Hattie the mouse's workshop tree stump. And so the traffic cones made a circle all the way around Hattie the mouse's little tree stump. And then they stopped and fell down because they were so tired. And all the traffic cones took a break for a minute and then they said, 'Hahaha that was fun. What should we do next, Hattie?' And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just got something stuck to my foot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You've got something stuck to your foot? Oh. So what did they try next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Playing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Playing. And Hattie... Hattie said 'Hmm, I wonder if I could think of a song. Does anybody know any songs?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I do. I know one. Caribbean Amphibian, a frog in a coconut tree. That's one they really liked a long time ago when they were really small.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Caribbean Amphibian. I like that song too. Caribbean amphibian, a frog in a coconut tree... (singing)... Frog in a coconut tree. And all of the traffic cones were dancing and dancing and dancing. And they had such a good time dancing to the Caribbean Amphibian song and then it was over and they were done dancing. And Mischief Mouse said 'Wow, that was a fun thing to do with our traffic cone friends. I wonder if they could make a stack.' What did the traffic cones say then?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know how to make a stack. Could you please teach me Mischief Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Mischief Mouse said 'Well, let's see, how do we make a stack? Well first we need a volunteer. That means one cone who says I'll be first.' Does anyone of the cones want to be the volunteer to be first?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ploplly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ploplly says 'Yes, I'll be the first cone. What do I do?' And Mischief Mouse said 'Well, just sit right here and hold still. Now I need a...' Whoops! Bonk! Haha that's okay, it was just the lantern falling down. Um, now we need a second cone to be a volunteer. Who's the second cone?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dolly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dolly cone. Okay, now you have an important job, which is to stack yourself on top of the first cone volunteer. You'll have to jump and plop right on top. Can you do it, Dolly?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dolly said, 'I don't know how to do this game.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well maybe I can give you some help. I'll pick you up and plop you on top. Are you ready? On the count of three. One, two, three, plop! And then they had a stack of two cones. And after that, the cones got the idea and they made a stack. Who was the next cone?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bolly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bolly was the third cone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Molly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Molly, Dolly. And then Molly, and then who else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Prolly... and then Stally... and then Tammy... and then Propsy... and then Pransoms... and then Holly... then Bro... and then Nestor... and then Prolam... and then Jom... and then Zo... and then Plo-Plorum. And there's friends.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, and then Mischief Mouse was standing there looking up at an incredibly tall stack of traffic cones thinking to herself 'Hmm, I wonder what happens next.' And Hattie the mouse said 'Well, your name is Mischief Mouse after all. I wonder what you could do with this very tall stack of cones.' And what did Mischief Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm standing on your hand. I'm standing on your elbow. I'm standing on your elbow. I'm standing on top of Dad.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So what did Mischief Mouse do? Did Mischief Mouse do some mischief?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She didn't? She didn't knock down the stack of cones?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, she just played cone. It's time to go everyone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh it's time to go everyone and so the cones all hopped down to the ground and they weren't a tower anymore because it was time to go home.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they were flowers. And then they were teddy bears. And then they were carrots. And then it was Izzy. And then all the flowers died. And it was poppy time. And it was Polly time and Dolly time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Well I think that is the end of our stars and stories.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Caribbean Amphibian
characters.description: Immortalized in the verses of a coastal song, the emerald frog perched amidst the coconut fronds is the transcendent architect of the world’s chronicle, a silent co-author who scratches out the narrative alongside the characters themselves.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.
